title: BS in Computer Science and Political Science - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
BS in Computer Science and Political Science - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
BS in Computer Science and Political Science
Apply now
Request info
The BS in Computer Science and Political Science combined major (Khoury College and the College of Social Sciences and Humanities) will give you an understanding of the role of technology in policymaking as you prepare to address global and societal challenges using computer science. Learn to respond to regional and global technological issues, examine pressing international security problems, and evaluate the agendas of national, international, and nongovernmental organizations.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program details
Career prospects
Career prospects
A combined major in computer science and political science will prepare you with valuable skills in these two increasingly important fields. Some possible career paths include:
Technology policy analyst
Cybersecurity policy analyst
Legal technology consultant
Government technology advisor
Technology researcher
Co-ops and internships
Co-ops and internships
Through co-op experiences, you will have opportunities to apply your skills and build connections in industry settings. Recent co-ops include:
Co-op at the National Security Agency, U.S. Government
Technology co-op at Johnson & Johnson
Data science co-op at John Hancock
Learn more about co-ops
Research opportunities
Research opportunities
You will have opportunities to partner with leading professors on active research projects. Our research areas include:
Artificial intelligence
Data science
Natural language processing and information retrieval
Explore research at Khoury
Learning outcomes
Learning outcomes
In this program, you’ll learn to:
Apply design principles in the  construction of software systems of varying complexity.
Use current techniques, skills, and tools necessary for effective and secure computing practice.
Apply mathematical foundations, algorithmic principles, and computer science theory in the modeling and design of computer-based systems in a way that demonstrates comprehension of the tradeoffs involved in design choices.
Campus location
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus
Applications and admissions
Deadlines
Deadlines
Learn more about our undergraduate early action, early decision, and regular decision options.
Explore deadlines
Degree costs
Degree costs
Pursuing a world-class education is an investment in your future. It’s also one of the most important decisions you’ll ever make. Visit our Undergraduate Admissions site to find current Northeastern University costs. You can also estimate the cost of your degree using our College Cost Estimator or Net Price Calculator.
Find and estimate degree costs
Financial aid and scholarships
Financial aid and scholarships
Northeastern University offers a range of financial aid and scholarship opportunities to help you fund your education. You’ll receive an initial financial aid package that outlines your options after admission. Once you enroll, Khoury College has multiple scholarship opportunities for current students to continue supporting your studies. We also encourage all students to submit the FAFSA to determine your eligibility for federal aid and loans.
Learn more about scholarship opportunities
Have questions?
Have questions?
Let us know if you have any questions about the application or enrollment process. We are here to help!
Contact an enrollment counselor
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
David Lazer
David Lazer is a University Distinguished Professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. His research investigates misinformation and political communication — especially on social networks — through the lens of computational social science.
Read bio
Visit the College of Social Sciences and Humanities
The BS in Computer Science and Political Science program is an interdisciplinary collaboration with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities.
Learn about the College of Social Sciences and Humanities
Get a head start on your graduate degree
In our accelerated master’s programs, you can start earning your master’s degree while you are still pursuing your undergraduate studies.
Browse accelerated BS+MS programs
